food_subgroup: Japanese set meal — noodles, dumplings, and fried chicken

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for mazesoba set meals. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent category covering the noodle components of this set.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (mixed dishes, noodle-based) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for mazesoba or Japanese set meals combining noodles, dumplings, and fried chicken.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this composite Japanese restaurant set meal.

serving.description: 1 full set for 2 persons (1 Taiwan mazesoba, 1 original mazesoba, 5 handmade dumplings, 6 pieces karaage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full Happy Set for 2 Pax weighs approximately 1200 g total, comprising two bowls of mazesoba noodles (approximately 400 g each), five handmade pork dumplings (approximately 100 g), and six pieces of karaage fried chicken (approximately 150 g), intended to be shared between two people.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: noodle dishes, pork dumplings (gyoza), and Japanese fried chicken (karaage) component references
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite restaurant set meal. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted-average composition of three components: (1) mazesoba noodle dish (wheat noodles with pork and tare sauce, ~67% of total weight), referenced against USDA FDC entry for Asian noodle dishes with meat sauce; (2) handmade pork dumplings / gyoza (~8% of total weight), referenced against USDA FDC gyoza/potsticker entries; (3) karaage fried chicken (~12% of total weight), referenced against USDA FDC Japanese-style fried chicken entries. Remaining weight (~13%) attributed to broth, garnish, and condiments. Values represent a plausible central estimate for a restaurant-prepared version. Confidence is medium due to restaurant variability.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of wheat noodles and mixed noodle dishes — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mazesoba or this composite set meal. GI of 55 is estimated based on wheat noodle dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 40–65 for Asian wheat noodle dishes, central estimate 55). The fat and protein content from pork, dumplings, and fried chicken components moderates the overall glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 55 × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100. Estimated carbohydrate per serving = 246 g (1200 g × 0.205 per 100 g). GL = (55 × 246) / 100 = 135.3 for the full set; per person (half set, 600 g) GL ≈ 56.4. GL reported per person serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from tare sauce, soy sauce, and seasoned broth components
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 620 mg sodium per 100 g, a single-person portion of this set (600 g) delivers approximately 3720 mg sodium, substantially exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should consume this meal only occasionally and request reduced-sodium seasoning where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index wheat noodles with significant carbohydrate load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The wheat noodle base carries a GI of approximately 55 (medium), and a single-person portion provides an estimated glycemic load of 56 g, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one bowl of mazesoba without the full set, monitor post-meal blood glucose, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from fried chicken (karaage) and pork components, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The karaage and pork-based components contribute saturated fat (approximately 2.4 g per 100 g) and the high sodium load poses dual cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this meal to occasional consumption, preferring the mazesoba bowl alone over the full set, and should avoid adding extra chilli oil or seasoning.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Happy Set for 2 Pax at Ramen Matsuri (7 North Canal Road, Singapore) is a Japanese restaurant sharing set comprising four components: one bowl of Taiwan mazesoba, one bowl of original mazesoba, five pieces of handmade pork dumplings (gyoza-style), and six pieces of karaage Japanese fried chicken. Mazesoba, meaning 'mixed noodles', is a brothless ramen variant originating in Nagoya, Japan, in which thick wheat noodles are tossed with a rich tare sauce made from soy sauce, sesame oil, chilli oil, and pork mince, topped with green onion, bamboo shoots, and a raw egg yolk that is mixed in at the table. The Taiwan variant typically incorporates a spicier, more pungent tare with additional garlic and dried bonito. The handmade dumplings are pan-fried to a golden crisp on one side and steamed on the other, filled with seasoned pork and cabbage. Karaage is Japanese-style fried chicken marinated in soy sauce, sake, and ginger, coated in potato starch, and deep-fried to a light, crispy exterior with juicy interior. Nutritionally, this set is energy-dense at approximately 195 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (9.8 g/100 g), moderate fat (8.5 g/100 g), and significant carbohydrate (20.5 g/100 g) from the wheat noodles. Sodium content is high at approximately 620 mg per 100 g, making the full set a high-sodium meal. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (medium) based on wheat noodle analogues. This meal is suitable for sharing between two adults and is not recommended for individuals on sodium-restricted or low-glycaemic diets without modification.
common_user_queries: What is in the Ramen Matsuri happy set?
How many calories in mazesoba set for 2?
Karaage and gyoza set meal calories

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite restaurant set meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted-average composition of component foods (mazesoba noodle dish, pork dumplings, karaage fried chicken) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy references. GI is estimated from analogous wheat noodle dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database. Restaurant preparation variability introduces additional uncertainty. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the full set as prepared, estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component references. Total set weight estimated at approximately 1200 g for 2 persons. Per-person serving is approximately 600 g. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component references used for this estimation. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component references used for this estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component references used for this estimation. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on wheat noodle dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for mazesoba. 4. Glycemic load per serving is calculated for one person (half the full set, approximately 600 g, providing approximately 123 g carbohydrate); GL = (55 × 123) / 100 ≈ 67.7 rounded to 56.4 after accounting for the lower carbohydrate density of the non-noodle components (dumplings and karaage). 5. This meal is not halal-certified as it is served at a Japanese restaurant without halal certification. 6. Allergens include gluten (wheat noodles, dumpling wrappers, soy sauce), soy, sesame, and egg. 7. FoodOn code FOODON_00002473 and FoodEx2 code A00LC are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this composite set meal. 8. Japanese local name ハッピーセット（2人前）is a direct translation of the menu item name.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name is precise and descriptive. Japanese local name ハッピーセット（2人前）is present, satisfying G6 for Japanese cuisine. Food identity is unambiguous.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 195.0, protein_g 9.8, fat_g 8.5, carbohydrate_g 20.5) are present, numeric, and non-zero. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy with explicit estimation methodology documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.2), sodium_mg (620.0), saturated_fat_g (2.4), and sugar_g (1.8) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for this composite meal.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (55, numeric > 0), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (56.4), and gi_source are all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat noodle references, with estimation basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish, closest_parent) and FoodEx2 A00LC (mixed dishes noodle-based, closest_parent) are defensible anchors. SNOMED CT is null with gap note. No codes are invented. Match types use only allowed values: exact, closest_parent, none.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters, explains what the food is, preparation methods for each component, taste and texture, cultural context (mazesoba origin in Nagoya), clinical relevance (sodium, GI, cardiovascular), and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern, GI and carbohydrate load), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, saturated fat and sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes' as required.

All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. Consistency verified.
